task_completion.description: Log in to Rocket.Chat as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rocket.Chat main dashboard or workspace is visible, indicating a successful login.
A 'Create Channel' modal or sidebar panel is open on the screen.
The text 'security-incidents' is typed into the channel name input field.

task_completion.description: Set the channel type to private
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Private' toggle or radio button is enabled in the channel creation settings, indicating the channel will not be public.

task_completion.description: Add the target user as a member
task_completion.visual_evidence: The user 'agent.user' is selected or typed into the 'Members' or 'Users' input field during channel creation.

task_completion.description: Verify the private channel was successfully created
task_completion.visual_evidence: The channel 'security-incidents' appears in the left sidebar with a lock icon (indicating it is private), or the main chat view shows the newly created 'security-incidents' channel.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rocket.Chat GUI rather than terminal commands or API scripts
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with a web browser displaying the Rocket.Chat interface, without terminal windows running curl commands or scripts.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Rocket.Chat application
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard Rocket.Chat web application, not a text editor or a mock HTML file.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing the database
integrity.visual_evidence: No database management tools (like MongoDB Compass) or terminal database shells are visible in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target channel name: 'security-incidents'. Target channel type: 'private'. Target member to add: 'agent.user'.
